title: Ozempic Is Doing Something to the Reward Circuit That Nobody Fully Planned For
description: Semaglutide was designed to lower blood sugar and shrink waistlines. Now a landmark Lancet Psychiatry study of 96,000 patients, a placebo-controlled JAMA Psychiatry trial, and data from over 4 million real-world users suggest it may also be suppressing addiction, depression, and anxiety — possibly by quieting the brain's dopamine reward circuit directly. The evidence is striking. The confounding is real. The counterevidence exists. And the implications are either transformative or a very expensive cautionary tale.
heroImageAlt: Editorial illustration for Ozempic Is Doing Something to the Reward Circuit That Nobody Fully Planned For
